name: how-a-bill-becomes-law
title: How a Bill Becomes a Law
desc: A seven-step process: a bill is introduced, studied in committee, passed by the full chamber, passed again by the other chamber, reconciled in conference if needed, signed or vetoed by the President, and becomes law.

steps.title: Introduced
steps.detail: A member introduces the bill in the House or Senate.

steps.title: Committee
steps.detail: A committee studies, amends, and votes on the bill.

steps.title: Floor vote
steps.detail: The full chamber debates and votes.

steps.title: Other chamber
steps.detail: The second chamber repeats the process.

steps.title: Conference
steps.detail: Differences between the two versions are reconciled.

steps.title: President
steps.detail: The President signs the bill or vetoes it.

steps.title: Becomes law
steps.detail: If signed — or a veto is overridden — it becomes law.
